caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the concept of liquid yield. The slide titled "Tutorial - 2" is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay. It includes a mathematical formula for liquid yield, \( y = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_f} \), which likely relates to the enthalpy changes during a thermodynamic process. On the right side of the slide, there is a T-S (temperature-entropy) diagram illustrating various thermodynamic processes such as constant temperature (T=const), constant pressure (p=const), and constant enthalpy (h=const). The diagram shows different paths labeled with numbers, indicating the sequence of states or processes being discussed. The cursor moves slightly between the two frames, suggesting that the presenter might be explaining specific points on the diagram or the formula. The overall content is technical and aimed at students or professionals studying cryogenic systems and their thermodynamic properties.
